edges.description: Loss of AGAT activity reduces production of guanidinoacetate, the creatine precursor.
Biallelic GATM loss directly reduces measurable tissue AGAT enzymatic activity.
Reduced AGAT product formation directly lowers guanidinoacetate in urine and plasma.
Impaired endogenous creatine synthesis lowers creatine in plasma and urine.
Low systemic creatine synthesis produces low or undetectable cerebral creatine stores.

edges.description: Low endogenous creatine synthesis also depletes skeletal muscle creatine stores.

edges.description: Brain creatine deficiency contributes to global developmental delay.

edges.description: Language delay is a common manifestation of cerebral creatine deficiency.

intermediate_mechanisms: Central and motor developmental consequences of creatine deficiency alter tone and motor function.
edges.source: Cerebral creatine depletion and impaired energy buffering

edges.description: Seizures are an expanded-spectrum manifestation of AGAT deficiency.
intermediate_mechanisms: Brain creatine depletion and associated structural or functional abnormalities can lower seizure threshold in some families.

nodes.description: Reduced creatine synthesis lowers brain creatine stores. Because the brain has high energy demand and limited reliance on peripheral creatine transport, cerebral creatine depletion disrupts phosphocreatine energy buffering, contributing to neurodevelopmental impairment, language delay, myopathy, and seizures in some families.
nodes.id: Cerebral creatine depletion and impaired energy buffering

nodes.description: Oral creatine monohydrate replenishes cerebral creatine stores and is the disease-directed treatment for AGAT deficiency. Published dosing ranges from 100 to 800 mg/kg/day, with serial MRS and biochemical monitoring used to assess brain creatine restoration. Early diagnosis is important because treatment can improve myopathy and may prevent adverse neurodevelopmental outcome when started in infancy; later treatment is still associated with partial cognitive or adaptive gains.

nodes.description: AGAT normally converts arginine and glycine to guanidinoacetate, which is methylated by GAMT to creatine. Reduced AGAT activity lowers guanidinoacetate and creatine in body fluids and tissues.
